mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month changes in industrial production (INDPRO) reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which boost expected corporate revenues and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes are not followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in > 55% of months, or the mean forward return is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.0% elevates the risk-free discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and increases debt-service costs for leveraged corporations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in > 50% of cases, or the mean return under this trigger is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal an overheating labor market that forces the central bank into a more restrictive stance, compressing equity valuation multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 0 and CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising PAYEMS and rising CPIAUCSL fails to produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in > 55% of triggered months, or the interaction term does not add marginal explanatory power beyond individual series (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In the immediate quarters following a GDP contraction, quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects recovery in aggregate demand and corporate revenue baselines, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 0 following two consecutive quarters of negative growth
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth in post-recession quarters coincides with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in > 50% of cases, or the mean forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail and food services sales (RSAFS) signals weakening household consumption expenditures, triggering immediate downward revisions in corporate revenue forecasts and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in RSAFS are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in > 50% of instances, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where the unemployment rate rises over a 3-month window while the federal funds rate falls over the same window signals a recessionary environment in which monetary easing fails to offset labor market deterioration, depressing earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising UNRATE and falling FEDFUNDS does not produce negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in > 55% of triggered periods, or the interaction coefficient is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate tightens short-term interbank liquidity and raises the marginal cost of capital, causing immediate risk-off repositioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in > 50% of instances, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals rapid labor market deterioration that reduces household income and consumption trajectories, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE increases exceeding 0.2 percentage points are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in > 50% of triggered months, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
